commit.message:
fix: preserve existing store data when URL only specifies a tab (#205)

Three related changes:

1. useUrlParams — query-param-only tab selection:
   Remove path-segment tab detection. Tab is now specified exclusively
   via ?tab=. Also fix the early-return: the old condition only returned
   early when no tab AND no params. Now the tab is applied first, then
   the remaining params are checked; if none are left the hook returns
   immediately — existing store values are untouched.

2. CustomPanel — sync local state with external store changes:
   Local phases state is initialised once via useState. If custom.phases
   in the store changed after that first render (e.g. useUrlParams setting
   phases from a URL, or Zustand async rehydration) the local state became
   stale and any interaction would write it back, overwriting the new data.
   Fix: useEffect re-syncs local state on every external change to
   custom.phases. An isInternalUpdate ref prevents the effect from firing
   on changes the component itself made via persist(), avoiding a loop.
   onPhasesChange is called after syncing so the timer display updates.

3. GitHub Pages SPA routing — simplify 404.html / index.html:
   The previous routing trick encoded the full URL path into the query
   string so index.html could restore it. With path-based tabs removed
   the path segment is irrelevant; 404.html now simply redirects to the
   app root preserving the query string and hash as-is. The corresponding
   restore script in index.html is removed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add SkipClassification flag to bypass scope classifier

New config option Loop.SkipClassification (default: false). When
enabled, the harness skips the classifier agent call and treats
all optimization queue items as narrow scope. Build + E2E tests
serve as the safety gate instead of the classifier.

This allows the harness to attempt architecture-classified
optimizations like database indexes without manual intervention.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: fallback C impl for routines f2c cannot convert (xerbla)

xerbla.f uses LEN_TRIM (Fortran 90 intrinsic), which f2c (Fortran 77
only) cannot handle, so f2c produces no xerbla.c. XERBLA's behaviour
is trivial and well-known (print error + return), so provide a
hardcoded f2c-ABI-compatible C implementation as a fallback.

Add _F77_FALLBACKS dict in s3_f2c.py keyed by lowercase file stem.
When f2c produces no .c output, check the fallback dict before
recording an error; if a fallback exists, write it as the .c file
and continue normally. Also covers xerbla_array.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: prefer stem-matching file when multiple files define same function

name_to_file was unconditionally overwritten on each occurrence.
Since files are sorted alphabetically, zblat3.f (z > x) was overwriting
xerbla.f for XERBLA, then pulling in zblat3.f as a 'dependency' of
DGEMM via XERBLA — which dragged in ZGEMM and all the BLAS test routines.

Fix: only overwrite name_to_file[name] when the new file's stem
(case-insensitive) matches the function name, or when there is no
existing entry. This ensures xerbla.f wins over zblat3.f for XERBLA,
and more generally that single-routine files are preferred over
combined/test files.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Potential fix for pull request finding

feat: progress spinner for list-all menu, C precision benchmark driver

menu.py:
- 'List all and select interactively' and 'Convert entire library' now
  show a rich spinner that updates with each file being parsed via status_fn

s2_benchmarks.py:
- Add generate_precision_dataset(): near-cancellation matrices with fixed
  seed 43, written as dataset_{fn}_precision_A/B.bin for reproducibility
- Update _make_dgemm_precision_driver(): reads shared dataset files
  (dataset_*_precision_A/B.bin) instead of unseeded RANDOM_NUMBER, so
  Fortran/C/Rust all operate on identical inputs
- Add _make_c_blas_precision_driver(): C near-cancellation benchmark that
  reads the shared precision dataset, calls the f2c-transpiled function,
  and writes bench_{fn}_precision_output.bin for cross-language comparison
- generate_benchmarks(): generates precision datasets in phase 1 for all
  KNOWN_BLAS functions; writes bench_{fn}_precision.c in phase 2 and adds
  it to bench_c_files so it flows through stages 3/4

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: detect EXTERNAL function calls parsed as Structure_Constructor

fparser2 parses calls to EXTERNAL-declared functions (like LSAME) as
Structure_Constructor nodes rather than Part_Ref or Function_Reference,
because it cannot determine at parse time whether LSAME is a function
or a derived-type constructor. Adding a walk over Structure_Constructor
with items[0] (the Type_Name) fixes detection of LSAME and similar
EXTERNAL functions. False positives are filtered by the BFS name_to_file
check. Verified: DGEMM now correctly resolves LSAME and XERBLA.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: detect function-call dependencies (e.g. LSAME) in stage 1

Previously only CALL_Stmt (subroutine calls) were collected. Function calls
in expressions like IF (LSAME(TRANSA,'N')) are parsed by fparser2 as
Part_Ref nodes — same syntax as array access. Now we also walk Part_Ref
and Function_Reference nodes and add their names as potential callees.
False positives (array subscripts) are harmlessly filtered out during BFS
because we only follow edges where the callee exists in name_to_file.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: dataset always generated first; LLM loops compile+accuracy only

Stage 2 datasets:
- Dataset generation is now a dedicated first pass, unconditionally before
  any benchmark compilation. Writes datasets.json as a separate manifest.
  Fortran benchmark compilation failure no longer affects dataset creation.

LLM repair loops (s4, s6, s7, s8):
- LLM loops now ONLY gate on: (a) compilation errors, (b) numerical accuracy
- Performance/timing is measured once after a successful build and stored in
  result.json for the report — no LLM loop triggered by performance
- s6: removed cargo-test bench gate; replaced with post-build timing + numerical
  diff capture (_run_bench) that records for the report but never triggers LLM
- s7/s8: added _console warning lines before each repair attempt showing
  what failed; raise MaxRetriesExceededError after exhausting retries

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: stage 3 benchmark conversion + failure reporting

Stage 3 / benchmarks:
- Benchmark .f drivers use Fortran 90/2003 (SYSTEM_CLOCK, ACCESS='STREAM',
  INTEGER PARAMETER ::) which f2c cannot handle — they were silently dropped
- Stage 2 now generates C benchmark drivers directly alongside the .f ones
  using the same numpy binary dataset, targeting the f2c calling convention
  (trailing underscore, ftnlen args for character parameters)
- Stage 3 pipeline no longer passes .f benchmarks to f2c; instead copies
  the pre-generated .c drivers into the stage 3 output dir where stage 4
  finds them via bench_*.c glob

Failure reporting (exceptions.py + stages):
- Add PipelineError hierarchy: CompilationError, NumericalPrecisionError,
  BenchmarkRuntimeError, ConversionError, MaxRetriesExceededError
- s4: print yellow warning line before each LLM repair showing what failed
  (file name + first error line for compile failures, max diff for precision)
- s4: raise MaxRetriesExceededError wrapping the specific cause after retries
- s6: add _console, _first_error_line, import exceptions (partial — s7/s8 pending)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: fire last visual cue when sleep overshoots scheduledTime (#204)

Action firing was only in the spin-wait (else) branch. When
remainingUntilAction was just barely above SPINWAIT_MS, the code slept
with sleepMs ≈ 0ms — but setTimeout has a minimum ~1-4ms resolution and
could wake past scheduledTime, hitting the break before the action was
ever fired.

Move postMessage({ type: 'action' }) out of the else branch into a
shared while (t >= nextAction) loop that runs after both paths. This
drains all due actions regardless of whether a sleep overshot, ensuring
the last visual cue always fires.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add in-memory API response caching with 15-min time bucketing

Query time ranges are bucketed to 15-minute boundaries (floor/ceil),
so photos taken within the same window reuse cached API responses
without making additional API calls. Cache is in-memory (module-level
table), keyed by icao_bucketFrom_bucketTo. Cache hits shown as
'⚡ Cached' in the search context header. Includes clearCache()
method for manual cache invalidation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix tab selection: use tab_view value binding instead of observers

Observers on per-tab dropdowns fire during dialog initialization,
clobbering the activeTab tracking. Instead, bind tab_view's value
property directly to activeTabId, which LR SDK updates when the
user switches tabs. Read the correct dropdown at dialog close based
on the active tab identifier.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix tab selection bug: read from active tab instead of cross-syncing

The old approach used observers to sync all tab selections to a single
selectedIndex property, but the 'All' tab observer would race and
overwrite selections from Arrivals/Departures tabs.

Now tracks which tab was last interacted with via activeTab property,
and reads the correct per-tab selection (selAll/selArr/selDep) when
the dialog closes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Refactor Mins Before display and fix calibrator rounding (#202)

- Rename TimerPanelHandle.createPhases() to createDisplayData() returning
  TimerPanelData { phases, minutesBeforeTarget: number | null }
- Use null to hide Mins Before field; number (including 0) to show it,
  fixing Gen 4/5 hiding the field when minutesBeforeTarget === 0
- Add getGen4MinutesBeforeTarget and getGen5MinutesBeforeTarget that compute
  minutes before target without calibration, so the displayed value reflects
  the raw target rather than calibration-adjusted timing
- Extract TimerPanelHandle/TimerPanelData types to src/components/timerPanel.ts
- Add tooltip to Mins Before field explaining its purpose
- Fix calibrator.ts rounding to match C# Math.Round (banker's rounding):
  - toDelays: Math.floor -> roundHalfToEven (was systematically under-counting)
  - toMilliseconds: unrounded float -> roundHalfToEven
  - calibrateToDelays precision mode: Math.round -> roundHalfToEven

commit.message:
Show API units and requests separately in quota display

AeroDataBox uses 'API units' (varies by endpoint tier) separately from
raw request count. Now parses both x-ratelimit-api-units-* and
x-ratelimit-requests-* headers. Display shows both when available:
'📊 API units: 580 / 600  •  Requests: 2360 / 2400'
Red warning threshold based on units (preferred) or requests as fallback.

commit.message:
fix: remove .npmrc legacy-peer-deps to fix rollup platform deps (#32)

The legacy-peer-deps flag causes npm to skip optional platform-specific
dependencies like @rollup/rollup-linux-x64-gnu, breaking the Vite build
on Linux CI runners.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs(architecture): reflect occlusion conditional GPU upload fix

- Added occlusion/ module directory (mod.rs + tests.rs) to module hierarchy
- Added occlusion uniform updates to Game Systems category
- Added 'Conditional GPU Uniform Updates' section to Performance Considerations
  documenting the two-level Local cache pattern, StaticOcclusionUniforms /
  DynamicOcclusionUniforms split, and the get_mut() guard rule
- Bumped version to 2.1.0, updated date to 2026-03-15

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: default PRs to draft

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix repeating EXC_BAD_ACCESS crash in PKMSync menu bar app (#136)

* Fix repeating EXC_BAD_ACCESS crash in PKMSync menu bar app

Remove .symbolEffect(.rotate) from MenuBarExtra label and popover
status header — continuous CoreAnimation on the label triggers
_NSWindowTransformAnimation lifecycle issues in AppKit's internal
window management, causing use-after-free during autorelease pool
drain.

Additional fixes:
- Use distinct SF Symbol for syncing state instead of animation
- Add orderOut(nil) before close() on diff window to suppress
  close animation and prevent stale animation references
- Remove double MenuBarViewModel initialization in PKMSyncApp
- Remove redundant startScheduler() call (already in MenuBarView.task)

* docs: fix Lambda function count and add macOS CI workflows

- README.md: 42 → 43 deployed Lambda functions
- CLAUDE.md: add macos-build.yml and macos-test.yml to workflows list

* Restore startScheduler() call at app init

The MenuBarView .task only fires when the popover is shown, so the
scheduler must be started at init for background syncing to work
without requiring the user to open the popover first.

* Potential fix for pull request finding

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs: add coding style and coding guardrails guides

coding-style.md — naming conventions, derive patterns, system
signatures, SystemSet registration, module organization, comment
rules, test organization, logging format, and formatting workflow.

coding-guardrails.md — 10 hard rules derived from real bugs:
unconditional get_mut, SpatialGrid requirement, delta clamping,
EditorHistory requirement, single active camera, cached SubVoxel
bounds, state-gated systems, SystemSet assignment, PartialEq-only
on float structs, and Local<T> scope.

investigate → requirements → architecture → user story →
code → tests → validate → commit.

known vs unknown root cause, existing ticket vs from scratch)
and Bevy/Rust-specific guidance for this repo.
